title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week Dry Bulk: Grain Dry Bulk flows from Russia to Turkey & Egypt
Rise in the first quarter of the year to a new high, with March recording the highest levels in the last two years
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform, Dry Bulk Flows https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/dry-dynamic-drybulkflows/2ns4zj/310311216?h=dK61XiFkJ1HZh7nEatsGvRnM2ODFNDVsRo4yHjyhWjc
The last few days of March brought positive momentum for the smaller vessel categories as we have begun to see clearer signs of demand growth, with grain flows supporting firmness in the second quarter of the year. We have already indicated that the extension of the Black Sea grain trade for the next few days in April and May will boost demand-side sentiment. However, port congestion and vessel supply will challenge the euphoria, as the number of ballast vessels in the Handysize segment is still excessive.

children.summary:
Rise in the first quarter of the year to a new high, with March recording the highest levels in the last two years
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-03-29T122925.354.png | assets/2023-03-29_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-03-29t122925354_ae718f4a2140.png
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform, Dry Bulk Flows https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/dry-dynamic-drybulkflows/2ns4zj/310311216?h=dK61XiFkJ1HZh7nEatsGvRnM2ODFNDVsRo4yHjyhWjc
The last few days of March brought positive momentum for the smaller vessel categories as we have begun to see clearer signs of demand growth, with grain flows supporting firmness in the second quarter of the year. We have already indicated that the extension of the Black Sea grain trade for the next few days in April and May will boost demand-side sentiment.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT - Market Rates ($/t) Firmer
children.summary:
‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-03-29T123214.574.png | assets/2023-03-29_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-03-29t123214574_01938bcc1f06.png
At the end of March, the firmness of the previous days was confirmed with a weakening momentum in Capesize and Panamax. Capesize vessel freight rates are now $20.5/tonne mark, up almost $4/tonne from 5 weeks ago.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY - Ballasters (# vessels) Decreasing
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-03-29T123305.049.png | assets/2023-03-29_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-03-29t123305049_d88f51127dcc.png
The number of ballast ships declined significantly in the major vessel categories, while Capesize ships maintained the accelerated pace of the previous week. Capesize SE Africa: The number of vessels now stands at 72, down 9 from the previous week and 8 below the annual average.

children.title: SECTION 3/ DEMAND - TonDays Increasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-03-29T123334.756.png | assets/2023-03-29_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-03-29t123334756_67f409981d20.png
Overall, March ends with a notable turnaround in the Panamax segment and improved sentiment in the smaller ship categories. Capesize demand ton-days: The upward trend of the previous three weeks slowed in the last days of March, and a loss of momentum is expected in the following days.

children.title: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION - No of Vessels Increasing Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
section_path: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION - No of Vessels Increasing Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
children.section_path_text: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION - No of Vessels Increasing Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-03-29T123412.415.png | assets/2023-03-29_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-03-29t123412415_4f2294f03e03.png
Congestion continues to increase, with the Handysize, Supramax and Panamax segments taking the current upward trend to a new accelerated level before the end of March. Capesize: The number of vessels now stands at 107, 14 more than in week 9.
